title: Fullstack Next.js 14 Application Authentication with NextAuth
description: Standard approach for securing an entire Next.js App Router application leveraging server actions, iron-session, or NextAuth (Auth.js) securely.
steps: 1. Install `next-auth@beta` for Next.js 14.
2. Create the `auth.ts` configuration file exporting auth, handlers, signIn, signOut.
3. Create the route handler in `app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`.
4. Setup a DB adapter (e.g. PrismaAdapter) if using Magic Links or Credentials.
5. Protect specific routes using Middleware (matching routes like `/dashboard/:path*`).
6. Retrieve session dynamically in Server Components using `await auth()`.
7. Use `<SessionProvider>` only if accessing session frequently on the Client.

title: OAuth and Magic Links for React + Node.js/Express Backend
description: Building a custom authentication system for a separated React SPA and Express Backend using Passport.js or a third-party service like Clerk/Supabase.
steps: 1. Decide whether to build natively (Passport.js/JWT) or use a managed service (Clerk/Supabase).
2. For managed: Install Clerk SDKs on both frontend (`@clerk/clerk-react`) and backend (`@clerk/clerk-sdk-node`).
3. Wrap React App with `<ClerkProvider>`.
4. Protect React routes using `<SignedIn>` and `<SignedOut>` components.
5. On the Express backend, apply the Clerk `requireAuth` middleware to restricted endpoints.
6. Pass the Authorization header containing the specific active session token from React.

title: CI/CD Pipeline Setup for Frontend Apps via GitHub Actions
description: A workflow for building, testing, and deploying frontend apps automatically.
steps: 1. Create a `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` file.
2. Define triggers: `on: push: branches: [ main ]`.
3. Set up the environment using `actions/setup-node`.
4. Add a step to run `npm ci` and `npm test`.
5. Add a step to run `npm run build`.
6. Use an action like `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages` or explicitly deploy to Vercel/Netlify via their CLI integrations.

title: Designing a Scalable Design System in Figma
description: Workflow for designing, testing, and scaling a fully-fledged design system using Figma variables and auto-layout.
steps: 1. Establish foundational tokens (colors, typography scales, spacing variables).
2. Create atomic components (buttons, inputs) heavily utilizing Auto Layout.
3. Build variants for interactive states (hover, active, disabled).
4. Leverage Figma's Component Properties to reduce structural duplication.
5. Create larger organism components combining atomic pieces.
6. Publish the library and document usage heavily.

title: End-to-end Testing with Playwright
description: Process for writing and executing robust, resilient E2E tests for web applications.
steps: 1. Install Playwright via `npm init playwright@latest`.
2. Configure `playwright.config.ts` targeting necessary browsers and base URLs.
3. Write tests focusing on user-flow rather than implementation details.
4. Utilize Playwright's Codegen feature to quickly map out user steps.
5. Run tests locally and debug using the `--ui` or `--debug` flags.
6. Integrate Playwright into your CI/CD pipeline.

title: AI-Powered SEO Content Strategy
description: Using AI agents to research, outline, and optimize long-form content for search ranking.
steps: 1. Analyze high-ranking competitors for a target keyword using Perplexity AI.
2. Extract primary and secondary keywords utilizing Surfer SEO.
3. Generate a comprehensive content outline with Claude 3.5 Sonnet.
4. Draft the article sections based on the outline using Jasper.
5. Use Hemingway Editor to simplify complex sentences and improve readability.
6. Finalize SEO optimization by matching Surfer SEO's top-suggested terms.

title: Automated Personal Branding on LinkedIn
description: Workflow for consistently generating and scheduling high-quality personal brand content.
steps: 1. Identify 5-10 core topics related to your expertise.
2. Generate 10-15 post ideas using ChatGPT or Claude.
3. Draft posts based on your favorite ideas utilizing Taplio or Buffer AI.
4. Repurpose long-form blog content into short LinkedIn posts with Opus Clip or Copy.ai.
5. Schedule your posts across the week using Buffer or Taplio.
6. Engage with comments on your posts daily to build community.

title: Rapid AI Website Prototyping
description: From a simple text description to a published, high-fidelity responsive website.
steps: 1. Draft the sitemap and wireframe layout with Relume AI.
2. Generate the visual design and content using Framer AI or Webflow AI.
3. Create custom brand imagery with Midjourney or Adobe Firefly.
4. Refine the UI components and responsiveness within the platform editor.
5. Connect a custom domain and SEO meta-tags.
6. Publish the site live to the web.

title: Low-Code AI Customer Support Pipeline
description: Building a system to automatically handle and resolve common customer queries using AI.
steps: 1. Centralize your knowledge base and documentation in Notion or a similar tool.
2. Connect your support channel (e.g. Email, Chat) to Intercom Fin or Zendesk AI.
3. Train the AI model on your existing FAQ and documentation.
4. Set up auto-reply rules for common queries like 'pricing' or 'login issues'.
5. Enable human hand-off for complex tickets or sentiment-based escalations.
6. Monitor AI resolution rates and update documentation based on common gaps.

title: Automated Market Research & Competitive Analysis
description: Using AI and web scraping to gather and synthesize market data on competitors.
steps: 1. Define the list of competitors and key metrics to track.
2. Use Browse AI or Apify to scrape competitor product pages and social media daily.
3. Send the scraped data to a centralized spreadsheet (Rows AI or Google Sheets).
4. Use Julius AI or ChatGPT to analyze trends, pricing changes, and feature gaps.
5. Generate a weekly summary report of competitive activity using Claude.
6. Use the insights to adjust your own product and marketing strategy.

title: AI Software Testing & Debugging Loop
description: Using AI to automatically identify, explain, and fix software bugs.
steps: 1. Identify a bug or a failing test case in your code editor.
2. Use Cursor's AI chat to explain the bug and suggest a potential fix.
3. Apply the suggested fix and run tests to verify implementation.
4. If tests still fail, use Phind or Sourcegraph Cody to research deeper causes.
5. Use Sweep AI to generate a pull request for the verified fix automatically.
6. Review the AI-generated PR and merge into the main codebase.

title: E-commerce Product Launch Workflow
description: Using AI to manage the content and marketing for a new product launch.
steps: 1. Generate product descriptions and ad copy using Hypotenuse AI or Anyword.
2. Create high-quality product lifestyle images using Midjourney.
3. Design launch social posts and email banners with Canva Magic Studio.
4. Automate launch day social media posts with Buffer or Loomly.
5. Track launch performance and customer feedback with HubSpot AI or Klaviyo AI.
6. Adjust ad creative and copy based on initial conversion data.

title: AI Video Content Factory
description: Producing high-quality short-form video content at scale for social media.
steps: 1. Draft video scripts based on high-performing blog posts using ChatGPT.
2. Generate video clips using Runway Gen-3 or Pika Labs from scripts.
3. Enhance video quality and add auto-subtitles with Veed.io or CapCut.
4. Add AI voiceovers to the clips using ElevenLabs.
5. Use Opus Clip to repurpose the final videos into 5-10 short social clips.
6. Schedule the clips for multi-channel distribution using Loomly.

title: Corporate Meeting Productivity Loop
description: Turning meetings into actionable documentation and tasks automatically.
steps: 1. Record and transcribe team meetings using Otter.ai or Fireflies.ai.
2. Use Fathom AI to generate high-level meeting summaries and action items.
3. Feed action items into ClickUp or Taskade for tracking.
4. Share the summarized meeting notes with the team via Slack or Microsoft Teams.
5. Use Notion AI to organize and store meeting outcomes in a knowledge base.
6. Review progress on action items in the next follow-up meeting.

title: AI Content Localization Workflow
description: Translating and adapting marketing content for global audiences rapidly.
steps: 1. Draft original marketing copy in English using Jasper or ChatGPT.
2. Use Claude to translate the copy into multiple target languages with proper tone.
3. Generate localized marketing videos with AI avatars using HeyGen.
4. Add localized AI voiceovers and subtitles to the videos using ElevenLabs.
5. Review localized content with native-speaking team members or agencies.
6. Launch targeted localized marketing campaigns across global regions.

title: AI-Driven Financial Prediction Model
description: Using data analytics and scenario planning to forecast business performance.
steps: 1. Consolidate historic financial and sales data into a centralized spreadsheet.
2. Use Causal to build a dynamic financial model based on key performance drivers.
3. Use Julius AI to run predictive analysis on the data for future trends.
4. Create different scenario models (Best, Worst, Average) for planning.
5. Visualize the predictions and scenarios for stakeholders with Polymer.
6. Update the model monthly with actual performance data for ongoing accuracy.

title: AI Knowledge Base for Product Teams
description: Building a centralized, AI-searchable repository for product documentation.
steps: 1. Import all product specs, roadmaps, and research notes into Notion.
2. Use Notion AI to summarize each document and generate meta-tags.
3. Enable Glean for enterprise-wide search across Notion and other team tools.
4. Use Mem to capture and organize ad-hoc meeting notes and observations.
5. Use ChatGPT or Claude to answer stakeholder questions using the documentation.
6. Regularly audit and update the knowledge base for accuracy.

title: Low-Code AI Recruitment Pipeline
description: Using AI and automation to streamline the hiring and candidate evaluation process.
steps: 1. Use ChatGPT to draft comprehensive job descriptions and interview questions.
2. Automate initial resume screening and ranking with specialized AI tools.
3. Schedule interview rounds automatically using Reclaim AI or similar calendar tools.
4. Record and transcribe interviews with Otter.ai for later review.
5. Use Claude to summarize interview feedback and rank candidates.
6. Manage the entire process from within your CRM or HR platform.

title: AI Product Prototyping for Founders
description: Going from zero to a working product prototype with minimum technical overhead.
steps: 1. Draft the product wireframe and layout using Uizard from a hand-drawn sketch.
2. Generate the design and initial front-end code with Framer AI or Lovable.
3. Create your early landing page for waitlist signups using Webflow AI.
4. Use Leonardo.ai or Midjourney to create stunning marketing visuals.
5. Iterate on the UI/UX based on early user feedback in Figma with Figma AI.
6. Use Bolt.new or Lovable to build and deploy your first functional MVP version.

title: Personalized AI Marketing Campaign
description: Using AI and automation to deliver highly tailored marketing messages.
steps: 1. Gather lead data and behavior through HubSpot AI or Typeform AI.
2. Segment leads based on interest and activity with Klaviyo AI.
3. Generate personalized email and ad copy for each segment using Anyword.
4. Deliver personalized email sequences through Klaviyo or Instantly AI.
5. Use Adobe Podcast to create short audio personalized messages for high-value leads.
6. Monitor engagement and conversion metrics across segments daily.
